description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

feature: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces
description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces?
Feature Caveat: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces - IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

description: In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for MACsec and UDLD?

Feature Caveat: MACsec and UDLD - In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

description: WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

description: In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

description: In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

description: MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

description: In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

description: Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels,

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for Multicast?
Feature Caveat: Multicast - Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels,.

description: Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for PFC?
Feature Caveat: PFC - Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for Traceroute?
Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

description: Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what limitation is mentioned for Traceroute?

Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

description: VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.
scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features.
workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: With the switch is configured with PTP protocol on multiple ports (both physical and lag) and user were to remove or add lag port to a clock source port, the sink port could flap. Scenario: In most cases, any PTP-related configuration changes will cause PTP services in the system to restart, which affects all the ports. Adding or removing a LAG port, can also cause PTP services to restart. There is a potential risk of of data traffic being miss-classified as PTP protocol packets for a very short window (milliseconds) and with enough of this traffic type, the PTP service will encounter the condition as if there were no sync packets from the grand source device. As a result, the PTP sink port will flap to re-establish the PTP protocol. However, even with the PTP sink port flap, the system can converge quickly and should not cause high offsets to downstream PTP clients. Workaround: Best practice is to implement PTP configuration changes during network downtime or a maintenance window.

description: Symptom: With the switch is configured with PTP protocol on multiple ports (both physical and lag) and user were to remove or add lag port to a clock source port, the sink port could flap. Scenario: In most cases, any PTP-related configuration changes will cause PTP services in the system to restart, which affects all the ports. Adding or removing a LAG port, can also cause PTP services to restart. There is a potential risk of of data traffic being miss-classified as PTP protocol packets for a very short window (milliseconds) and with enough of this traffic type, the PTP service will encounter the condition as if there were no sync packets from the grand source device. As a result, the PTP sink port will flap to re-establish the PTP protocol. However, even with the PTP sink port flap, the system can converge quickly and should not cause high offsets to downstream PTP clients. Workaround: Best practice is to implement PTP configuration changes during network downtime or a maintenance window.
symptom: With the switch is configured with PTP protocol on multiple ports (both physical and lag) and user were to remove or add lag port to a clock source port, the sink port could flap.

Adding or removing a LAG port, can also cause PTP services to restart. There is a potential risk of of data traffic being miss-classified as PTP protocol packets for a very short window (milliseconds) and with enough of this traffic type, the PTP service will encounter the condition as if there were no sync packets from the grand source device. As a result, the PTP sink port will flap to re-establish the PTP protocol. However, even with the PTP sink port flap, the system can converge quickly and should not cause high offsets to downstream PTP clients.
workaround: Best practice is to implement PTP configuration changes during network downtime or a maintenance window.

description: Symptom: Growth in the boot.log file file size may lead to an increase in RAM utilization. Scenario: This issue can be triggered by a high rate of unauthorized or wrong requests to the REST HTTPS server, which could occur during malicious attacks or vulnerability tool tests.

description: Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

description: Symptom: Some hosts may experience connectivity issues. Scenario: In an EVPN environment with ARP suppression enabled, random hosts could experience connectivity issues due to missing the associated ARP entry. Workaround: Temporarily add andremove a static ARP entry using the following commands Copy interface vlan <vlan_id>
